1.name: Phase 1 — The Dashboard
1.deliverable: Tactical bridge: Go TUI over Python's api_server HTTP+SSE boundary

items.name: Orchestrator failure-row stabilization for 4-8 workers
items.note: P0: stop false failure rows when codex exits non-zero despite valid final report + single clean commit; preserve promotion flow and keep loop throughput viable at higher parallelism.

items.name: Planner wrapper/test consistency closeout
items.note: TDD: reconcile the current wrapper policy before treating the planner-task-manager rename as fully stable. The worktree deletes scripts/gormes-architecture-task-manager.sh and scripts/architectureplanneragent.sh while internal/architectureplanneragent_test.go and docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-gormes-architecture-task-manager-script.md still expect compatibility wrappers; either restore tiny exec wrappers or update tests/docs to a single-entrypoint policy, then run the internal script tests.

2.name: Phase 2 — The Gateway
2.deliverable: Go-native operator wiring harness: tools, Telegram, shared gateway chassis, shipped cron, and the first OS-AI spine slices before the long-tail adapter flood

items.name: Slack Socket Mode adapter
items.note: TDD landed: internal/slack owns a Socket Mode bot with AuthTest, event acking, message filtering, placeholder updates, and final/error delivery over the kernel.

items.name: Thread routing + coalesced reply flow
items.note: TDD landed: internal/slack preserves reply thread timestamps, coalesces streaming updates through a placeholder, and persists channel/thread session handles.

items.name: Slack CommandRegistry parser wiring
items.note: TDD: route Slack slash/message command ingress through gateway.ParseInboundText and the shared CommandRegistry, with adapter-local affordances kept behind Slack-specific tests.

items.name: Slack gateway.Channel adapter shim
items.note: TDD: adapt internal/slack onto the gateway.Channel interface and Manager lifecycle without rewriting the existing Socket Mode client or coalesced reply tests.

items.name: Slack config + cmd/gormes gateway registration
items.note: TDD: add Slack config loading, doctor coverage, and cmd/gormes gateway registration only after the Channel shim is green; current evidence shows only Telegram and Discord are registered there.

items.name: Bridge-vs-native runtime decision
items.note: TDD: port the runtime-selection seam from upstream `gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py` into a Go contract that freezes bridge-first vs native-first startup, session-path ownership, and bot/self-chat policy before transport wiring. Current tree only lands `internal/channels/whatsapp.NormalizeInbound`.

items.name: Inbound normalization + command passthrough
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/whatsapp.NormalizeInbound normalizes bridge/native peer IDs and routes generic slash commands through gateway.ParseInboundText.

items.name: Pairing, reconnect, and send contract
items.note: TDD: add a transport-neutral outbound lifecycle contract that gates sends on pairing state, retries reconnects with bounded backoff, and maps normalized gateway chat IDs back to raw WhatsApp DM/group peers with reply metadata preservation.

items.name: Gateway session store + SessionSource parity
items.note: TDD landed: gateway session-ID resolution now prefers stored handles, falls back deterministically to the chat-scoped key on empty/error paths, and freezes SessionSource/session-context prompt contracts in gateway tests.

items.name: SessionContext prompt injection
items.note: TDD landed: each inbound submit now injects a stable Current Session Context system block ahead of recall/user messages, covering source metadata plus deterministic delivery-target exposure.

items.name: DeliveryRouter + --deliver target parsing
items.note: TDD landed: --deliver parsing now covers origin/local/platform/platform:chat/platform:chat:thread forms with explicit invalid-shape tests before future adapter routing binds them to live channels.

items.name: Gateway stream consumer for agent-event fan-out
items.note: TDD landed: a typed GatewayStreamConsumer contract now replays one render frame to multiple delivery targets in deterministic order and preserves later deliveries when one sink fails.

items.name: Inbound event normalization + session identity
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/signal.NormalizeInbound now freezes Signal DM/group chat IDs, generic slash passthrough, and alternate phone/UUID session identity before client wiring.

items.name: Reply/send contract on shared chassis
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/signal.Bot now records reply metadata from normalized inbound events and routes shared-chassis sends back to direct recipients or native group IDs with reply-to preservation.

items.name: Signal transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze signal-cli or bridge client lifecycle, receive-loop reconnect/error handling, and outbound attachment/send seams before binding a real transport.

items.name: Email ingress + outbound delivery contract
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/email now normalizes RFC 822 MIME bodies into plaintext gateway events and derives deterministic reply-thread targets before IMAP or SMTP transport glue.

items.name: SMS ingress + outbound delivery contract
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/sms.NormalizeInbound now canonicalizes phone-number session keys, delegates generic slash commands through gateway.ParseInboundText, and BuildDelivery splits outbound replies into natural-boundary 1600-rune segments for future Twilio sends.

items.name: Threaded text adapter contract suite
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/threadtext freezes canonical thread IDs, gateway inbound normalization, and reply-target derivation before Matrix/Mattermost transport work.

items.name: Matrix shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD: land a Matrix-specific bot seam on top of `internal/channels/threadtext` that freezes canonical thread reply targets, root-message thread defaults, and placeholder/edit hooks before auth/sync/E2EE transport work.

items.name: Mattermost shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD: land a Mattermost-specific bot seam on top of `internal/channels/threadtext` that freezes canonical reply targets, flat-vs-thread root reply defaults, and placeholder/edit hooks before REST/WS transport work.

items.name: Matrix real client/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze auth, sync/invite handling, room-kind and mention policy, and media/E2EE bootstrap seams before binding a real Matrix client.

items.name: Mattermost REST/WS bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze auth, websocket reconnect, channel/user lookup, and file upload/edit seams before binding a real Mattermost transport.

items.name: Signed event parsing + auth gates
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/webhook.ParseInbound now enforces body-size and event-filter gates, and ValidateSignature freezes GitHub, GitLab, and generic HMAC auth before any HTTP runtime wiring.

items.name: Prompt-to-delivery routing bridge
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/webhook now renders prompt and deliver_extra templates, derives deterministic webhook session chat IDs, and resolves typed gateway delivery targets including thread aliases.

items.name: BlueBubbles + HomeAssistant adapters
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/bluebubbles and internal/channels/homeassistant now freeze webhook-auth, cached home-channel delivery, filtered state-change formatting, cooldown, and persistent-notification contracts before any SDK wiring.

items.name: Feishu shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/feishu now pins webhook-versus-websocket ingress, group mention policy, allowlist behavior, and reply-target preservation before any SDK wiring.

items.name: WeCom + WeiXin shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/wecom and internal/channels/weixin now pin policy-gated ingress, request/reply routing, and per-platform reply-path contracts before any SDK wiring.

items.name: DingTalk shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/dingtalk.Bot now covers allow-list policy, mention-gated ingress, generic command parsing, session-webhook capture, and callback-bound reply delivery; runtime/bootstrap and real SDK binding remain tracked separately.

items.name: QQ Bot shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/qqbot now covers DM/group policy gates, mention-gated ingress, and passive-reply sequencing before any runtime bootstrap.

items.name: Feishu transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze webhook and websocket client lifecycle, credential validation, and rich-text send failure modes before binding a real Feishu transport.

items.name: Feishu drive-comment rule + pairing seam
items.note: TDD: port the upstream `gateway/platforms/feishu_comment_rules.py` contract as a Go rule resolver with exact document, wildcard, and top-level fallback plus allowlist/pairing decisions before comment replies can invoke an agent.

items.name: Feishu drive-comment reply workflow
items.note: TDD: freeze `drive.notice.comment_add_v1` parsing, OK-reaction behavior, whole-document vs local-thread prompt construction, and reply fallback semantics from `gateway/platforms/feishu_comment.py` before real SDK binding.

items.name: WeCom + WeiXin transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze callback/webhook bootstrap, credential validation, and outbound push/reply lifecycle seams before binding real WeCom or WeiXin transports.

items.name: DingTalk transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/dingtalk now freezes Stream Mode bootstrap via DecideRuntime plus SessionWebhooks refresh and ReplySender retry/error contracts before any real SDK binding.

items.name: DingTalk real SDK binding
items.note: TDD: bind the frozen Stream Mode/runtime contract to the real DingTalk SDK, credential checks, receive loop, and send lifecycle without widening the already-tested session-webhook retry seam.

items.name: QQ Bot transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze gateway/event receive loops, passive-reply lifecycle, and platform-specific send failure handling before binding a real QQ transport.

items.name: Architecture planner tasks manager script
items.note: TDD landed for the main tracked entrypoint: scripts/gormes-architecture-planner-tasks-manager.sh writes .codex/planner/architecture-planner-tasks.md plus report/state artifacts, logs verbose repo/upstream/progress/validation/schedule context, and installs a periodic systemd/cron run. Wrapper compatibility is tracked separately in Phase 1.C because current docs/tests and worktree state disagree about legacy wrapper presence.

2.E.1.name: OS-AI Spine: Delegation Policy + Child Execution

items.name: Runner-enforced tool allowlists + blocked-tool policy
items.note: TDD landed: blocked-tool and allowlist tests now cover the runner execution seam before any child tool call reaches the executor.

items.name: Tool-call audit in typed child results
items.note: TDD landed: manager/result and delegate_task output tests now require typed child tool-call events to survive into terminal result envelopes.

items.name: Real child Hermes stream loop
items.note: TDD landed: HermesRunner now executes a real child OpenStream loop with token/output streaming, multi-iteration tool_calls continuation through executeChildTool, explicit stream-open error handling, and registry wiring that opts delegate_task into the real runner whenever a live Hermes client is available.

items.name: Canonical CommandDef registry
items.note: TDD landed: internal/gateway now owns one CommandRegistry with ResolveCommand, ParseInboundText, registry-derived help output, and canonical alias handling for /start -> /help.

items.name: Gateway slash dispatch + per-platform exposure
items.note: TDD landed: Telegram and Discord parse through the shared gateway command layer, the manager welcome/help text is registry-driven, and deterministic Telegram/Slack exposure helpers now derive from the same source of truth.

items.name: Gateway per-event hook registry
items.note: TDD landed: manager lifecycle tests now lock before/after receive, direct and placeholder-backed before/after send, and on_error hook emission on the shared gateway boundary.

items.name: Hook manifest discovery + handler loading
items.note: TDD landed: HOOK.yaml directories now load executable command handlers with exact, prefix-wildcard, and global event matching, and `gormes gateway` wires the XDG hooks root into the live manager.

items.name: Built-in BOOT.md startup hook
items.note: TDD landed: gateway startup now reads $XDG_DATA_HOME/gormes/BOOT.md, runs it through an isolated background boot kernel, and treats missing files or boot execution failures as non-blocking startup conditions.

items.name: Graceful restart drain + managed shutdown
items.note: TDD landed: manager shutdown tests now lock drain-until-idle, shutdown timeout, submit rejection during shutdown, and signal-driven root-context cancellation only after the drain path returns.

items.name: Adapter startup failure cleanup contract
items.note: TDD: mirror upstream GatewayRunner._safe_adapter_disconnect by proving partially-started channel resources are closed when Connect/Run startup returns false or errors, and that cleanup errors are logged without masking the original startup failure.

items.name: Active-turn follow-up queue + late-arrival drain policy
items.note: TDD: decide and freeze Gormes's active-turn follow-up semantics before adding richer interrupt/queue behavior; upstream BasePlatformAdapter now keeps the active-session guard live across pending drains and spawns a late-arrival drain task so follow-ups cannot duplicate agent runs or disappear during cleanup.

items.name: Drain-timeout resume_pending recovery
items.note: TDD: add a session read-model flag for restart/shutdown drain timeouts that preserves the session_id and injects a reason-aware resume note on the next turn, while marking only agents still running at timeout and letting hard suspended/stuck-loop state override resume_pending.

items.name: Pairing read-model schema + atomic persistence
items.note: TDD: port the minimal read model from upstream `gateway/pairing.py` into an XDG-backed `pairing.json` with atomic writes, deterministic ordering, and per-platform paired/unpaired state before any approval UX.

items.name: Pairing approval + rate-limit semantics
items.note: TDD: freeze code generation, expiry, max-pending, failed-attempt lockout, and per-user request rate limiting from upstream PairingStore before adapters start issuing pairing codes.

items.name: `gormes gateway status` read-only command
items.note: TDD: add a read-only `gormes gateway status` surface over the shared pairing/runtime model so operators can inspect configured channels plus paired/unpaired state without starting transports.

items.name: Runtime status JSON + PID/process validation
items.note: TDD: port the upstream gateway_state.json/PID-file read model with stale PID cleanup and process identity checks before wiring live channel state.

items.name: Token-scoped gateway locks
items.note: TDD: port upstream `gateway/status.py` acquire_scoped_lock/release_scoped_lock behavior into an XDG state lock directory keyed by platform credential hash, with stale PID/start-time cleanup, stopped-process detection, and release-on-shutdown coverage before multi-profile adapters share credentials.

items.name: Gateway /restart command + takeover markers
items.note: TDD: add a registry-backed gateway /restart path with restart notify/dedup markers, service-manager restart exit semantics, and planned --replace takeover-marker handling so redelivered Telegram restart updates cannot loop a fresh process.

items.name: Channel lifecycle writers into status model
items.note: TDD: thread live channel lifecycle updates into the shared pairing/status surface and prove read-model coherence before adapter-specific pairing UX widens.

items.name: Home channel ownership rules
items.note: TDD: define home-channel selection, explicit override precedence, and per-platform fallback rules before cross-platform delivery code.

items.name: Notify-to delivery routing
items.note: TDD: prove delivery-target expansion from notify-to and home-channel settings before adapter dispatch starts consulting directory state.

items.name: Channel directory atomic persistence + lookup
items.note: TDD: port upstream `gateway/channel_directory.py` as an atomic `channel_directory.json` read model with composite `chat_id:thread_id` keys and human name/type lookup before periodic refresh or operator UX.

items.name: Channel directory refresh + stale-target invalidation
items.note: TDD: add deterministic refresh/stale-target behavior after manager-source persistence exists, so send-target resolution never depends on adapter-specific cache side effects.

items.name: Manager remember-source hook
items.note: TDD: persist allowed inbound SessionSource records through gateway.Manager before periodic refresh or send-target resolution starts depending on the directory.

items.name: Mirror + sticker cache surfaces
items.note: TDD: prove cache key and mirror-write contracts before adapter-specific caches land.

3.name: Phase 3 — The Black Box (Memory)
3.deliverable: SQLite + FTS5 + ontological graph + semantic fusion in Go; 3.E closes session visibility, audit trails, decay, and cross-chat/session boundaries

items.name: Read-only bbolt sessions.db -> index.yaml mirror
items.note: TDD: shipped internal/session SessionIndexMirror with fixed YAML shape, lexically ordered bbolt key export, and atomic temp-file rewrite semantics.

items.name: Deterministic mirror refresh without mutating session state
items.note: TDD: shipped a deterministic background refresh loop plus TUI/gateway/telegram runtime wiring, skipping unchanged snapshots while keeping the bbolt session map read-only.

items.name: Append-only JSONL writer + schema
items.note: TDD: shipped internal/audit JSONL append writer with a stable envelope (timestamp/source/session_id/agent_id/tool/args/duration_ms/status/result_size_bytes/error) and order-preserving tests.

items.name: Kernel + delegate_task audit hooks
items.note: TDD: kernel executeToolCalls now emits audit records, and delegated child-tool execution under delegate_task appends the same JSONL schema through the subagent runtime.

items.name: Outcome, duration, and error capture
items.note: TDD: RED/GREEN coverage locks duration_ms, status, and error population for successful and failed tool executions, including delegated child-tool runs.

items.name: gormes session export <id> --format=markdown
items.note: Shipped with Cobra wiring and clean missing-session handling; tests pin CLI shape and not-found behavior.

items.name: Render turns, tool calls, and timestamps from SQLite
items.note: Markdown export now reads SQLite turns, renders deterministic ordering/timestamps, and includes persisted tool-call metadata.

items.name: gormes memory status command
items.note: TDD: shipped `gormes memory status` with a SQLite-backed extractor summary, heuristic worker health, and recent dead-letter rendering.

items.name: Extractor queue depth + dead-letter summary
items.note: TDD: added deterministic dead-letter error aggregation to `gormes memory status`, with fixture-backed queue, summary, and CLI rendering coverage.

items.name: Append-only daily usage.jsonl writer
items.note: TDD landed: internal/insights now appends normalized DailyRollup records to usage.jsonl with schema-locked JSONL lines and deterministic append ordering.

items.name: Session, token, and cost rollups from local runtime
items.note: TDD: shipped fixture-driven daily rollups plus a telemetry.Snapshot bridge for session counts, token totals, cost sums, and per-model breakdowns.

items.name: relationships.last_seen schema + backfill
items.note: TDD landed: schema v3g adds relationships.last_seen, backfills legacy rows from updated_at, and keeps recall decay on COALESCE(NULLIF(last_seen, 0), updated_at) so migrated and fresh rows rank deterministically.

items.name: Relationship writer freshness updates
items.note: TDD landed: graph relationship upserts now advance last_seen on every observation while preserving the legacy updated_at timestamp for structural freshness semantics.

items.name: Deterministic weight attenuation at recall time
items.note: TDD landed: recall-time decay is now wired through RecallDecayHorizonDays, fixed-clock recall_sql coverage, and ranking assertions that preserve raw fence weights while attenuating candidate ordering.

items.name: user_id concept above chat_id
items.note: TDD landed: internal/session now persists per-session source/chat/user metadata, resolves canonical user_id bindings above chat_id, inherits known chat bindings for resumed sessions, and rejects conflicting chat-to-user merges.

items.name: Same-chat default recall fence
items.note: TDD landed: internal/memory recall applies a same-chat default fence to exact, FTS, and semantic seed selection, so named entities from another chat do not leak without explicit scope widening.

items.name: Opt-in user-scope recall + source filters
items.note: TDD landed: cross-chat recall widens only through canonical user_id bindings and optional source allowlists, with unresolved user scope falling back to same-chat behavior.

items.name: Honcho-compatible scope/source tool schema
items.note: TDD: make honcho_search and honcho_context schemas advertise optional scope and sources while preserving external Honcho-compatible names and keeping omitted fields same-chat by default.

items.name: Cross-chat deny-path fixtures
items.note: TDD: prove unknown, unresolved, or conflicting user_id bindings cannot widen recall or session search, and pin same-chat fallback behavior with fixture-backed allow/deny cases before cross-chat access is exposed as shipped.

items.name: Cross-chat operator evidence
items.note: TDD: add operator-readable evidence for allowed and denied cross-chat recall paths, including resolved user_id/source scope details, before marking 3.E.7 complete.

items.name: parent_session_id lineage for compression splits
items.note: Execution plan frozen: add parent_session_id + lineage_kind to session.Metadata, keep roots null/primary by default, reject trivial loops, and surface orphan chains in mirrors/status instead of failing silently.

items.name: Source-filtered session/message search core
items.note: TDD landed: internal/memory.SearchMessages and SearchSessions search canonical user_id-bound sessions with optional source allowlists, deterministic latest-turn ordering, and split source/chat key normalization.

items.name: GONCHO user-scope search/context parameters
items.note: TDD landed: internal/goncho SearchParams and ContextParams accept scope=user plus sources[] and pass them into the session catalog; the Honcho-compatible tool schemas still need to advertise those fields.

items.name: Lineage-aware source-filtered search hits
items.note: TDD: once parent_session_id exists, make SearchMessages/SearchSessions expose parent/child lineage context for matched sessions without changing default same-chat recall behavior.

items.name: Operator-auditable search evidence
items.note: TDD: add CLI or mirror evidence that shows source filters, user scope, and lineage metadata for session/message search so operators can audit why a cross-source hit appeared.

4.name: Phase 4 — The Brain Transplant
4.deliverable: Native Go agent orchestrator + prompt builder
4.dependency_note: Build priority: 2.E0 deterministic subagent runtime -> 2.G static skills + reviewed candidate flow -> runner-enforced delegation policy + wider gateway surface -> native agent loop

items.name: Provider interface + stream fixture harness
items.note: TDD: `internal/hermes/client.go` already freezes shared ChatRequest/Message/Event/ToolCall contracts and Anthropic+Bedrock tests exercise them; the remaining closeout is a reusable fixture harness that replays provider transcripts, usage, and finish reasons across adapters before Gemini/OpenRouter/Codex land.

items.name: Tool-call normalization + continuation contract
items.note: TDD: streamed tool-call accumulation and tool-result continuation payload mapping are already shared across Anthropic/Bedrock; the remaining closeout is cross-provider fixture coverage that locks assistant tool-call messages, tool replies, and continuation-safe finish reasons so later adapters cannot diverge silently.

items.name: Anthropic
items.note: TDD landed in `internal/hermes`: direct Anthropic Messages API request shaping now preserves cache-control metadata on system/tool-result blocks, streams `thinking`/`text`/`input_json_delta` events into the shared event model, maps Anthropic stop reasons to continuation-safe finish reasons, and covers 429/rate-limit fixtures with `httptest` instead of live credentials.

items.name: Bedrock
items.note: TDD landed in `internal/hermes`: the native Bedrock ConverseStream adapter now resolves region defaults, delegates AWS credential loading and SigV4 request signing to the AWS SDK, maps canonical system/user/assistant/tool messages into Bedrock tool-aware conversation payloads, streams reasoning/text/tool-use deltas back into the shared event model, and translates Bedrock API error codes into retry-safe `HTTPError` classifications without requiring live AWS credentials in tests.

items.name: Gemini
items.note: TDD: port `agent/gemini_cloudcode_adapter.py` with content-part normalization, tool-call mapping, safety/error payload handling, and no live Google credential dependency in unit tests.

items.name: OpenRouter
items.note: TDD: port `agent/openrouter_client.py` as an OpenAI-compatible adapter variant, proving base URL/model selection, provider headers, retryable error mapping, and usage accounting.

items.name: Google Code Assist
items.note: TDD: port `agent/google_code_assist.py` only after OAuth/token-store seams are testable; freeze request shaping, quota/error handling, and credential refresh behavior with fake token providers.

items.name: Codex
items.note: TDD: add a Codex provider adapter on the shared stream contract, including developer-message role handling, tool-call deltas, and explicit unsupported-feature errors before native Codex routing is allowed.

items.name: Long session management
items.note: Umbrella only: keep for inventory continuity; execute through the context-engine interface, token-budget status, context-reference, and compression slices below.

items.name: Context compression
items.note: Umbrella only: do not execute as one slice. Preserve cache-safe history semantics while the smaller compressor tasks land.

items.name: ContextEngine interface + status tool contract
items.note: TDD: port the `agent/context_engine.py` interface, `get_status` payload, update_model_context behavior, and unknown tool error shape before any compressor implementation is wired into the agent loop.

items.name: Compression token-budget trigger + summary sizing
items.note: TDD: port threshold calculation, context-length probing, summary token sizing, and ineffective-compression cooldown from `agent/context_compressor.py` with fixed token-count fixtures.

items.name: Tool-result pruning + protected head/tail summary
items.note: TDD: freeze old tool-output pruning, protected system/first-turn head messages, recent-tail preservation, and summary prefix compatibility before mutating live message history.

items.name: Manual compression feedback + context references
items.note: TDD: port `manual_compression_feedback.py` and `context_references.py` as separate read/write contracts so user-visible compression feedback and reference handles are testable without a provider call.

items.name: System + memory + tools + history assembly
items.note: Umbrella only: execute through the context-file, role/model guidance, skills snapshot, and memory/session-search prompt slices before replacing the Python bridge.

items.name: Context-file discovery + injection scan
items.note: TDD: port SOUL/HERMES/AGENTS/CLAUDE/.cursorrules discovery order, 20k-character truncation, YAML frontmatter stripping, and prompt-injection/invisible-character scan behavior from `agent/prompt_builder.py`.

items.name: Model-specific role and tool-use guidance
items.note: TDD: freeze developer-vs-system role selection plus OpenAI/Codex/Gemini/Google operational guidance blocks so provider adapters receive stable prompt roles.

items.name: Toolset-aware skills prompt snapshot
items.note: TDD: port skill manifest hashing, in-process/disk snapshot cache, tool/toolset condition filtering, and deterministic ordering before dynamic skills enter native prompt assembly.

items.name: Memory and session-search guidance assembly
items.note: TDD: assemble memory guidance, session-search guidance, available tool-name gates, and environment/platform hints without changing existing Phase 3 GONCHO recall semantics.

items.name: Model metadata registry + context limits
items.note: TDD: port `agent/model_metadata.py` and `agent/models_dev.py` into a read-only Go registry with deterministic context-window, pricing, capability, and provider-family fixtures before routing decisions consume it.

items.name: Routing policy and fallback selector
items.note: TDD: port `agent/smart_model_routing.py` as a pure selector over task signals, explicit overrides, provider availability, and fallback_model behavior before any provider call can switch models.

items.name: Per-turn model selection
items.note: TDD landed a turn-scoped `PlatformEvent.Model` override in `internal/kernel`, carrying the selected model across tool iterations while request/frame/telemetry state reflects the active turn and `cfg.Model` remains the fallback for later turns.

items.name: Trajectory writer + redaction gates
items.note: TDD: port `agent/trajectory.py` as an opt-in writer with deterministic message/tool snapshots, secret redaction, and failure-is-nonfatal behavior before broad telemetry capture.

items.name: Self-monitoring telemetry
items.note: TDD: bridge native turn/provider/tool metrics into the existing Phase 3 insights rollup without changing the local `usage.jsonl` schema until compatibility tests pass.

items.name: Title prompt and truncation contract
items.note: TDD: port `agent/title_generator.py` prompt inputs, length caps, empty-history fallback, and provider-error fallback with fake model responses before persistence wiring.

items.name: Auto-naming sessions
items.note: TDD: update session-title persistence only after the title contract is green, proving titles are generated once, can be manually overridden, and do not mutate historical transcript content.

items.name: Token vault
items.note: TDD: port credential-file discovery and storage from `tools/credential_files.py` into XDG-scoped paths with permission, missing-file, and profile-isolation tests before providers read tokens.

items.name: Multi-account auth
items.note: TDD: port `agent/credential_pool.py` account selection and rotation semantics with fake credential records before any provider adapter relies on account pools.

items.name: Google OAuth flow + refresh seam
items.note: TDD: port `agent/google_oauth.py` as a transport-neutral OAuth state machine with device/browser flow fixtures, refresh-token persistence, and cancellation/error cases before Google Code Assist uses it.

items.name: Provider-side resilience
items.note: TDD landed in `internal/hermes` + `internal/kernel`: provider HTTP failures now classify rate-limit/auth/context signals from status + body, surface capped `Retry-After` hints on `HTTPError`, and feed the kernel's open-stream retry delay selection so 429/slowdown responses back off deterministically without live provider credentials in tests.

items.name: Classified provider-error taxonomy
items.note: TDD: `internal/hermes/errors.go` already classifies retryable vs fatal HTTP/auth/context/rate-limit conditions for kernel retries; the remaining closeout is a richer structured taxonomy comparable to `agent/error_classifier.py`, including explicit rate-limit/auth/context/non-retryable envelopes and broader provider fixture tables.

items.name: Retry-After + jittered backoff policy
items.note: TDD landed in `internal/hermes/errors.go` + `internal/kernel/retry.go`: Retry-After hints parse from headers/body, delays are capped, the reconnect budget uses 1s/2s/4s/8s/16s backoff with +/-20% jitter, and kernel open-stream retries combine provider hints with that schedule under tests.

items.name: Prompt-cache capability guard
items.note: TDD: port `agent/prompt_caching.py` model/provider capability checks so cache-control metadata is attached only when the selected provider supports it.

items.name: Provider rate guard + budget telemetry
items.note: TDD: port `agent/rate_limit_tracker.py` and `agent/nous_rate_guard.py` as deterministic budget/rate decisions that feed Phase 3 insights without sleeping in unit tests.

5.name: Phase 5 — The Final Purge
5.deliverable: Python tool scripts ported to Go or WASM

items.name: 61-tool registry port
items.note: Umbrella only: keep for inventory continuity. Execute via registry inventory, pure/core tools, stateful filesystem tools, network tools, and adapter-specific tool groups rather than one bulk port.

items.name: Tool registry inventory + schema parity harness
items.note: TDD: snapshot upstream `tools/registry.py`, `toolsets.py`, and discovered schemas into a Go parity fixture that proves names, toolsets, required env vars, and JSON result shapes before porting handlers.

items.name: Pure core tools first
items.note: TDD: port side-effect-light tools such as todo, clarify, debug helpers, and local formatting helpers before network/browser/sandbox tools so the native registry can grow without external credentials.

items.name: Stateful tool migration queue
items.note: TDD: group file/session/checkpoint/process tools by shared state contracts and prove XDG/path isolation plus rollback semantics before exposing write-capable tools to the native agent loop.

items.name: Environment interface + file sync contract
items.note: TDD: port `tools/environments/base.py` and `file_sync.py` into one Go interface with path mapping, upload/download, timeout, and cleanup fixtures before backend-specific code lands.

items.name: Docker
items.note: TDD: port Docker execution after the environment interface is green, covering image selection, mount policy, env passthrough allowlists, timeout cleanup, and blocked dangerous mounts.

items.name: Modal
items.note: TDD: freeze Modal request/job lifecycle, upload/download mapping, and unavailable-credential behavior as fixtures before any cloud execution path is enabled.

items.name: Daytona
items.note: TDD: port Daytona workspace lifecycle with fake API clients, proving create/reuse/delete decisions and workspace path mapping before live credentials are required.

items.name: Singularity
items.note: TDD: port Singularity command construction, bind mounts, and timeout cleanup as a local command builder first; live runtime tests stay opt-in.

items.name: Browser action contract + event transcript
items.note: TDD: port the `tools/browser_tool.py` action schema, page-state transcript, screenshot/result envelopes, and unavailable-backend errors before choosing Chromedp or Rod.

items.name: Chromedp
items.note: TDD: bind Chromedp behind the browser action contract with fake page fixtures for navigation, click/type, screenshot, and timeout behavior before live browser smoke tests.

items.name: Rod
items.note: TDD: keep Rod as a second backend only after the shared contract is stable, proving backend selection and parity for core actions without diverging schemas.

items.name: Browser provider bridge + Firecrawl fallback
items.note: TDD: port `tools/browser_providers/{browserbase,firecrawl}.py` as optional provider adapters with credential gating and deterministic fallback/error results before network-backed browsing is default.

items.name: Multimodal in/out
items.note: Umbrella only: split into image input normalization, vision request routing, image-generation request/result storage, and safety/error envelopes before exposing multimodal tools.

items.name: Vision input normalization + token budget
items.note: TDD: port `tools/vision_tools.py` image path/URL/base64 normalization, size/token estimation, and unsupported-provider errors using local fixture images.

items.name: Image generation result contract
items.note: TDD: port `tools/image_generation_tool.py` schema, output-path handling, binary/result envelope, and provider error mapping before any live image model call.

items.name: Voice mode port
items.note: Umbrella only: split voice-mode state, transcription ingestion, TTS synthesis, and platform delivery hooks before enabling voice across gateways.

items.name: Transcription tool contract
items.note: TDD: port `tools/transcription_tools.py` input validation, provider selection, and text/error result shape with local audio fixtures before gateway media hooks call it.

items.name: TTS synthesis + voice-mode state
items.note: TDD: port `tools/tts_tool.py`, `tools/voice_mode.py`, and `tools/neutts_synth.py` behind a sidecar-friendly interface with cached samples and nonfatal missing-dependency behavior.

items.name: Skills hub
items.note: TDD: port `tools/skills_hub.py` and registry lookup as read-only search/install planning first, reusing the Phase 2.G active/inactive store instead of creating another skills root.

items.name: Skill registries
items.note: TDD: port clawhub/lobehub/hermes-index registry metadata with cache, signature, and unavailable-network fixtures before write-capable install flows.

items.name: MCP client
items.note: TDD: port `tools/mcp_tool.py` as a lifecycle-managed client with server discovery, schema normalization, timeout/cancel behavior, and deterministic fake MCP servers.

items.name: OAuth flows
items.note: TDD: port `tools/mcp_oauth.py` and `mcp_oauth_manager.py` as a reusable OAuth state store with redirect/device fixtures before managed MCP gateways request tokens.

items.name: Managed tool gateway bridge
items.note: TDD: port `tools/managed_tool_gateway.py` after the MCP client and OAuth store are green, proving tool discovery and result pass-through without a live external server.

items.name: ACP server side
items.note: TDD: port `acp_adapter/` as a protocol server with auth, session lifecycle, tool permission prompts, and event streaming fixtures before editor integrations are advertised.

items.name: Plugin SDK
items.note: TDD: port plugin manifest loading, capability registration, version checks, and isolation boundaries from `plugins/` before third-party extension execution.

items.name: Third-party extensions
items.note: TDD: add extension install/enable/disable flows only after the SDK contract is stable, with fixture plugins proving failure containment and no implicit runtime code execution.

items.name: Dangerous action gating
items.note: Umbrella only: do not execute as one slice. Use the detector, approval-mode, cron-mode, and policy-integration tasks below before dangerous terminal/file/code tools become available.

items.name: Dangerous-command detector + blocked-result schema
items.note: TDD: port upstream dangerous-command pattern detection into a pure Go guard that returns the same blocked tool-result shape agents already understand, without prompting or side effects.

items.name: Approval mode config normalization
items.note: TDD: add config parsing for manual/smart/off approval modes, including YAML/TOML boolean edge cases such as bare off, before wiring prompts or smart auxiliary review.

items.name: Cron dangerous-command approval mode
items.note: TDD: track upstream approvals.cron_mode drift by marking cron-fired turns as non-interactive and default-denying dangerous commands with an adaptive blocked message, while allowing an explicit approve mode to preserve old auto-approve behavior.

items.name: Tirith, path, URL, and website policy integration
items.note: TDD: compose external security findings with dangerous-command warnings into one guard decision so gateway/cron/CLI replays cannot bypass one check after only another was approved.

items.name: Sandboxed exec
items.note: TDD: port `tools/code_execution_tool.py` on top of the Phase 5.B environment interface, proving language selection, stdout/stderr limits, timeout, and blocked filesystem/network behavior.

items.name: Atomic checkpoints
items.note: TDD: port `tools/{file_operations,file_tools,checkpoint_manager,patch_parser}.py` as checkpoint-first read/write/patch primitives with rollback and path-policy fixtures before agent-visible writes.

items.name: Multi-model coordination
items.note: TDD: port `tools/mixture_of_agents_tool.py` only after provider routing and subagent envelopes are stable, using fake child model outputs to prove fan-out, aggregation, and failure isolation.

items.name: Todo
items.note: TDD: port `tools/todo_tool.py` as a small stateful tool with schema/result parity, deterministic persistence, and no dependency on the full file-tools port.

items.name: Clarify
items.note: TDD: port `tools/clarify_tool.py` as an interruptible prompt contract that preserves gateway/TUI response routing and times out safely in non-interactive cron turns.

items.name: Session search
items.note: TDD: port `tools/session_search_tool.py` on top of the Phase 3 session catalog, preserving same-chat defaults and opt-in user/source filters.

items.name: Debug helpers
items.note: TDD: port `tools/debug_helpers.py` as read-mostly diagnostic tools with redaction and no hidden writes before exposing them to production agents.

items.name: Cronjob tool API + schedule parser parity
items.note: TDD: port `tools/cronjob_tools.py` action/update/list surface plus `cron/jobs.py` once, interval, cron-expression, repeat-count, one-shot recovery, and stale recurring fast-forward behavior as fixtures over the Go cron store.

items.name: Cron prompt/script safety + pre-run script contract
items.note: TDD: port critical prompt threat scanning, invisible Unicode rejection, script-path confinement under the scripts directory, and configurable pre-run script timeout before exposing cron creation to tools.

items.name: Cron multi-target delivery + media/live-adapter fallback
items.note: TDD: port comma-separated deliver targets, home-channel/channel-directory resolution, response wrapping, MEDIA tag extraction, and live-adapter-first delivery fallback from `cron/scheduler.py` without changing the Phase 2.D MVP audit store.

items.name: 49-file CLI tree port
items.note: Umbrella only: keep for inventory continuity, but execution should use smaller command/config/platform/diagnostic slices rather than attempting the whole hermes_cli tree at once.

items.name: CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy
items.note: TDD: expand the Go command registry toward upstream hermes_cli/commands.py and prove every recognized gateway slash command either has a safe running-agent handler or returns a visible busy response without interrupting or queuing discardable command text.

items.name: Config, profile, auth, and setup command surfaces
items.note: TDD: port hermes_cli config/profile/auth/setup/uninstall flows as separate command groups with XDG-safe state paths and no implicit writes outside the selected Gormes home.

items.name: Gateway, platform, webhook, and cron management CLI
items.note: TDD: port operator management commands after the shared gateway read models land, so status/pairing/platform/cron commands consume the same stores as the runtime.

items.name: Diagnostics, backup, logs, and status CLI
items.note: TDD: port read-mostly diagnostics and backup surfaces independently from mutating setup/auth commands, with fixture-backed output tests before live filesystem integration.

items.name: OCI image
items.note: TDD: mirror upstream Docker entrypoint/config volume behavior with container smoke tests that run `gormes doctor --offline` and avoid bundling Python runtime paths.

items.name: Homebrew
items.note: TDD: add formula/release artifact checks after the static binary and docs install paths are stable, with version, checksum, and install layout fixtures.

items.name: SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI
items.note: TDD: port the tui_gateway server/render/slash-worker event contract so remote Bubble Tea clients can subscribe to live agent state without the Python gateway process.

items.name: OpenAI-compatible chat-completions API server
items.note: TDD: port the upstream gateway/platforms/api_server.py /v1/chat/completions surface with API-key auth, body-size limits, OpenAI content-part normalization, X-Hermes-Session-Id continuity, and streaming/non-streaming error-envelope fixtures before exposing the native Go brain over HTTP.

items.name: Responses API store + run event stream
items.note: TDD: port ResponseStore as a bounded persistent read model, previous_response_id chaining, GET/DELETE /v1/responses, POST /v1/runs, GET /v1/runs/{run_id}/events SSE, and orphaned-run cleanup as fixtures before external OpenAI-compatible clients depend on stateful runs.

items.name: API server health + cron admin endpoints
items.note: TDD: port /health/detailed plus /api/jobs list/create/update/delete/pause/resume/trigger after the Phase 5.N cronjob store/API parity slices land, reusing the same auth and OpenAI-style error envelope instead of creating a second cron control plane.

6.name: Phase 6 — The Learning Loop (Soul)
6.deliverable: Native skill extraction. Compounding intelligence. The feature Hermes doesn't have.

items.name: Heuristic or LLM-scored signal
items.note: TDD: start with deterministic local signals from transcript length, tool-call count, retries, edits, and operator feedback before adding any LLM scorer, so learning-loop triggers are explainable.

items.name: LLM-assisted pattern distillation
items.note: TDD: define the extraction input bundle and output schema with fake model fixtures, including rejection of secrets, one-off tasks, and implementation-specific noise before live generation.

items.name: Portable SKILL.md format
items.note: TDD: extend the Phase 2.G parser/store contract with versioned metadata, provenance, review state, and atomic writes while preserving human-editable SKILL.md files.

items.name: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup
items.note: TDD: combine lexical tags with Phase 3 semantic embeddings behind a deterministic scorer, proving disabled/unreviewed skills stay excluded from prompt injection.

items.name: Skill effectiveness scoring
items.note: TDD: persist skill-use outcomes, explicit operator feedback, and decay/weight adjustments as auditable records before any automatic promotion or demotion behavior.

items.name: TUI + Telegram browsing
items.note: TDD: expose browse/edit/disable/review flows through TUI and gateway commands after the store and feedback records are stable, with permission and active-turn behavior pinned by tests.
